_description: Tested SQL query templates for common clinical trial questions. Used by the MCP server to expose the aact://query-patterns resource. These patterns help LLMs generate correct, efficient SQL by providing proven examples.

patterns.intent: List planned endpoints (outcome measures) for trials matching a condition
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about endpoints, outcome measures, primary/secondary endpoints for recruiting or any trials
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, do_ut.outcome_type, do_ut.measure, do_ut.time_frame
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.design_outcomes do_ut ON s.nct_id = do_ut.nct_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'RECRUITING'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
ORDER BY s.nct_id, do_ut.outcome_type
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Uses design_outcomes (Protocol domain), NOT outcomes (Results domain). Safe for recruiting trials.

patterns.intent: List posted results/measurements for completed trials
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about actual results, efficacy data, or outcome measurements from completed trials
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, o.title AS outcome_title, o.outcome_type,
       om.classification, om.category, om.param_type, om.param_value_num, om.units
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcomes o ON s.nct_id = o.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcome_measurements om ON o.id = om.outcome_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'COMPLETED'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Uses outcomes + outcome_measurements (Results domain). Only works for completed trials with posted results.

patterns.intent: List study sites/locations for trials matching a condition
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about sites, locations, centers, or where trials are conducted
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, f.name AS facility_name, f.city, f.country, f.status
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.facilities f ON s.nct_id = f.nct_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'RECRUITING'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
ORDER BY f.country, f.city
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Facilities table is Protocol domain, always populated.

patterns.intent: List trials with their interventions for a condition
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about drugs, treatments, or interventions for a disease
patterns.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, i.intervention_type, i.name AS intervention_name
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.interventions i ON s.nct_id = i.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
ORDER BY s.nct_id
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Add UPPER(s.overall_status) filter if user specifies a status.

patterns.intent: Find trials by sponsor or funder
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about trials sponsored by a company or funded by NIH/industry
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, UPPER(s.overall_status) AS status, sp.name AS sponsor_name, sp.agency_class
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.sponsors sp ON s.nct_id = sp.nct_id
WHERE sp.lead_or_collaborator = 'lead'
  AND sp.name ILIKE '%{sponsor}%'
ORDER BY s.study_first_submitted_date DESC
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Filter on lead_or_collaborator = 'lead' to get the primary sponsor only.

patterns.when_to_use: User asks about who can participate, age limits, gender, inclusion/exclusion criteria
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, e.gender, e.minimum_age, e.maximum_age, e.healthy_volunteers
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.eligibilities e ON s.nct_id = e.nct_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'RECRUITING'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: eligibilities has one row per study. gender values are UPPERCASE: ALL, FEMALE, MALE.

patterns.when_to_use: User asks about randomization, blinding, study design, masking
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, UPPER(s.phase) AS phase, d.allocation, d.intervention_model, d.masking, d.primary_purpose
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.designs d ON s.nct_id = d.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: designs has one row per study.

patterns.intent: Get adverse events for completed trials
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about adverse events, safety data, side effects
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, re.event_type, re.organ_system, re.adverse_event_term,
       re.subjects_affected, re.subjects_at_risk
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.reported_events re ON s.nct_id = re.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
  AND re.event_type = 'serious'
ORDER BY re.subjects_affected DESC
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Results domain only. reported_events is only populated for trials with posted results.

patterns.intent: Count trials by status for a condition
patterns.when_to_use: User asks how many trials exist, trial counts, or status distribution
patterns.sql:
SELECT UPPER(s.overall_status) AS status, COUNT(DISTINCT s.nct_id) AS trial_count
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
GROUP BY UPPER(s.overall_status)
ORDER BY trial_count DESC;
patterns.notes: No LIMIT needed for aggregation queries.

patterns.intent: Count trials by phase for a condition
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about phase distribution of trials
patterns.sql:
SELECT UPPER(s.phase) AS phase, COUNT(DISTINCT s.nct_id) AS trial_count
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
GROUP BY UPPER(s.phase)
ORDER BY trial_count DESC;
patterns.notes: Phase values are UPPERCASE without spaces: PHASE1, PHASE2, PHASE3, PHASE4, EARLY_PHASE1, NA, PHASE1/PHASE2, PHASE2/PHASE3.

patterns.intent: Find trials in a specific country
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about trials in a country or region
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, UPPER(s.overall_status) AS status, c.name AS country
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.countries c ON s.nct_id = c.nct_id
WHERE cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
  AND c.name ILIKE '%{country}%'
  AND c.removed = FALSE
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: countries table has one row per country per study. Use removed = FALSE to exclude countries where sites were removed.

patterns.intent: Get study arms with their interventions
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about treatment arms, what each arm receives, comparators
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, dg.group_type, dg.title AS arm_title,
       i.intervention_type, i.name AS intervention_name
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.design_groups dg ON s.nct_id = dg.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.design_group_interventions dgi ON dg.id = dgi.design_group_id
JOIN ctgov.interventions i ON dgi.intervention_id = i.id
WHERE s.nct_id = '{nct_id}'
ORDER BY dg.group_type, dg.title;
patterns.notes: Uses the three-way join through design_group_interventions to link arms to their interventions.

patterns.intent: Get statistical analyses with p-values for an outcome
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about statistical results, p-values, confidence intervals, treatment effects
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, o.title AS outcome_title,
       oa.param_type, oa.param_value, oa.p_value, oa.method,
       oa.ci_lower_limit, oa.ci_upper_limit
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.outcomes o ON s.nct_id = o.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcome_analyses oa ON o.id = oa.outcome_id
WHERE s.nct_id = '{nct_id}'
ORDER BY o.title;
patterns.notes: Results domain only. outcome_analyses links to outcomes via outcome_id.

patterns.intent: Get baseline demographics (age, sex) per treatment arm for completed trials
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about participant demographics, baseline characteristics, age/sex breakdown by arm, or baseline data comparison across groups
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, rg.title AS arm_title,
       bm.title AS measure_name, bm.category, bm.param_type,
       bm.param_value_num, bm.units
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.result_groups rg ON s.nct_id = rg.nct_id
  AND rg.result_type = 'Baseline'
JOIN ctgov.baseline_measurements bm ON rg.id = bm.result_group_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'COMPLETED'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
  AND bm.title IN ('Age', 'Sex: Female, Male', 'Race/Ethnicity, Customized')
ORDER BY s.nct_id, rg.title, bm.title
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Uses the result_groups bridge pattern: filter rg.result_type = 'Baseline' (Title Case) to get baseline-specific arms. Common bm.title values include 'Age', 'Sex: Female, Male', 'Race/Ethnicity, Customized'. Results domain only — only populated for trials with posted results.

patterns.intent: Compare adverse events across treatment arms for completed trials
patterns.when_to_use: User asks to compare adverse events between arms, safety comparison between treatment and control, or adverse event rates per group
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, rg.title AS arm_title,
       re.event_type, re.organ_system, re.adverse_event_term,
       re.subjects_affected, re.subjects_at_risk
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.result_groups rg ON s.nct_id = rg.nct_id
  AND rg.result_type = 'Reported Event'
JOIN ctgov.reported_events re ON rg.id = re.result_group_id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'COMPLETED'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
  AND re.event_type = 'serious'
ORDER BY s.nct_id, rg.title, re.subjects_affected DESC
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Uses result_groups with result_type = 'Reported Event' to separate AE data by arm. The rg.title column identifies each arm (e.g. 'Drug A 10mg', 'Placebo'). Compare subjects_affected/subjects_at_risk across arms to calculate incidence rates. Change event_type to 'other' for non-serious AEs.

patterns.intent: Get efficacy outcome measurements broken down by treatment arm
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about outcome results per arm, efficacy data by treatment group, or comparing measurement values across arms
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, o.title AS outcome_title, o.outcome_type,
       rg.title AS arm_title,
       om.param_type, om.param_value_num, om.units,
       om.dispersion_type, om.dispersion_value_num
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcomes o ON s.nct_id = o.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcome_measurements om ON o.id = om.outcome_id
JOIN ctgov.result_groups rg ON om.result_group_id = rg.id
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'COMPLETED'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
  AND UPPER(o.outcome_type) = 'PRIMARY'
ORDER BY s.nct_id, o.title, rg.title
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Demonstrates the dual-join pattern: outcome_measurements links to outcomes via outcome_id AND to result_groups via result_group_id. This gives per-arm efficacy data for each outcome. Filter outcome_type for primary endpoints (UPPERCASE values: PRIMARY, SECONDARY, OTHER_PRE_SPECIFIED, POST_HOC). Results domain only.

patterns.intent: Track participant flow — enrollment, completion, and dropout reasons per arm
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about how many participants completed the study, dropout rates, withdrawal reasons, or participant retention by arm
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, rg.title AS arm_title,
       ms.title AS milestone, ms.period, ms.count AS milestone_count,
       dw.reason AS withdrawal_reason, dw.count AS withdrawal_count
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.conditions cond ON s.nct_id = cond.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.result_groups rg ON s.nct_id = rg.nct_id
  AND rg.result_type = 'Participant Flow'
LEFT JOIN ctgov.milestones ms ON rg.id = ms.result_group_id
LEFT JOIN ctgov.drop_withdrawals dw ON rg.id = dw.result_group_id
  AND dw.period = ms.period
WHERE UPPER(s.overall_status) = 'COMPLETED'
  AND cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%{condition}%'
ORDER BY s.nct_id, rg.title, ms.period, ms.title
LIMIT 100;
patterns.notes: Uses result_groups with result_type = 'Participant Flow' for flow-specific arms. LEFT JOINs milestones and drop_withdrawals because not all arms have dropouts. Common milestone titles: 'STARTED', 'COMPLETED'. The period column groups milestones into study phases (e.g. 'Overall Study'). drop_withdrawals shares the same period values and links through result_group_id.

patterns.intent: Get complete statistical analysis results with arm identification
patterns.when_to_use: User asks about treatment comparison statistics with arm details, which arms were compared in the analysis, or full outcome analysis pipeline
patterns.sql:
SELECT s.nct_id, s.brief_title, o.title AS outcome_title,
       oa.param_type AS estimate_type, oa.param_value AS estimate_value,
       oa.p_value, oa.method AS statistical_method,
       oa.ci_n_sides, oa.ci_lower_limit, oa.ci_upper_limit,
       rg.title AS compared_arm, rg.ctgov_group_code
FROM ctgov.studies s
JOIN ctgov.outcomes o ON s.nct_id = o.nct_id
JOIN ctgov.outcome_analyses oa ON o.id = oa.outcome_id
JOIN ctgov.outcome_analysis_groups oag ON oa.id = oag.outcome_analysis_id
JOIN ctgov.result_groups rg ON oag.result_group_id = rg.id
WHERE s.nct_id = '{nct_id}'
ORDER BY o.title, oa.id, rg.ctgov_group_code;
patterns.notes: Chains through the full results hierarchy: studies -> outcomes -> outcome_analyses -> outcome_analysis_groups -> result_groups. This is the ONLY way to identify which arms were compared in each statistical analysis. outcome_analysis_groups is a bridge table — each analysis typically has 2 rows (one per compared arm). The param_type in outcome_analyses is free text (e.g. 'Mean Difference', 'Odds Ratio (OR)', 'Hazard Ratio (HR)').

sql_conventions: Always prefix tables with ctgov schema: ctgov.studies, ctgov.conditions, etc.
Use UPPER() on both sides or ILIKE for comparisons on enum-like columns (overall_status, phase, gender, study_type) because values are stored in UPPERCASE.
Use cond.downcase_name ILIKE '%term%' for condition searches (already lowercase).
Never use SQL reserved words as table aliases (DO, AS, GROUP, ORDER, etc.). Use descriptive aliases: do_ut for design_outcomes, cond for conditions, grp for result_groups, sp for sponsors.
Add LIMIT 100 unless the user explicitly requests more rows or the query is an aggregation.
Use COUNT(DISTINCT s.nct_id) when counting trials to avoid inflated counts from many-to-one joins.
When joining multiple many-per-study tables, consider using DISTINCT to avoid row multiplication.

When joining Results-domain tables through result_groups, filter rg.result_type to scope the groups. Values are Title Case: 'Baseline', 'Outcome', 'Reported Event', 'Participant Flow'. Not all joins require this filter — joins through outcome_measurements or outcome_analysis_groups already constrain the group type.
